1998-12-30.abstract: Profile of scene in Hamilton, Scotland; Hamilton is first community in Britain to impose curfew on its young people, with special child safety officers questioning anyone they know to be under age of 16 on streets after eight PM and taking them home if they cannot supply sound reason why they are out; curfew measure has met with almost unanimous approval of parents; studies show it has cut theft and vandalism, but curfew is mainly aimed at curbing more of cheeky kid variety of misconduct, youths on street corners marking out territory, smoking and drinking cheap wine; photo; map (M)

1998-12-30.title: Long War Saps Spirit And Money In Congo
1998-12-30.abstract: Residents of Kinshasa, Congo, once euphoric, are increasingly weary of nation's war against rebels, war that has drawn in seven of Congo's neighbors; Congo Pres Laurent Kabila refusees to meet with rebels; many in Kinshasa think officials should meet with rebels; photo; map (M)

1998-12-30.title: Vow of Neutrality In Indonesia Election
1998-12-30.abstract: Gen Wiranto, head of Indonesia's armed forces, pledges that military will be neutral and not try to influence national elections next year (S)

1998-12-30.title: World Briefing
1998-12-30.abstract: EUROPE    BRITAIN: CHILD PROSTITUTE AS VICTIM -- The Government issued guidelines aimed at treating child prostitutes as victims of abuse rather than as criminals and at marking their employers and clients for prosecution. The latest annual figures, for 1995-1996, show that 177 youths under 17 were convicted of prostitution and another 207 were formally cautioned.   Warren Hoge (NYT)    BRITAIN: BATONS VS. TRUNCHEONS -- The British police are being warned that the growing substitution of longer and heavier American-style batons for the 11-inch wooden truncheons traditionally carried by officers has resulted in an increased number of brutality complaints. The report from the independent Police Complaints Authority says British police forces should instruct their members in restrained use of the new weapons to reduce the possibility of serious injury.   Warren Hoge (NYT)

1998-12-31.title: Netanyahu Challenged From Left And Right
1998-12-31.abstract: Israel's Environment Minister, Rafael Eitan, becomes fourth right-wing candidate to throw his hat into ring to replace Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu; many important issues face country, but so far, only lightning rod for debate has been Netanyahu's character; Netanyahu sees race as between him and 'the left,' term he uses as slur; but now he faces opposition from his own side; even Ariel Sharon, hawkish Foreign Minister and Netanyahu's most powerful ally, would not categorically rule out run for Prime Minister; photo (M)

1998-12-31.title: Non-Orthodox Rabbis Win A Court Skirmish in Israel
1998-12-31.abstract: Chief judge of Jerusalem District Court orders Israeli Government to recognize conversions to Judaism performed by non-Orthodox rabbis; decision is expected to reawaken divisive issue of who is a Jew; Orthodox Jewish politicians denounce ruling and promise to circumvent it by bringing bill to Parliament that confers sole right to perform conversions on Orthodox rabbinate; leaders of minority Reform and Conservative movements celebrate what they consider landmark victory in battle to win equal status for their movements in Jewish homeland, where Orthodox control religious life (M)

1998-12-31.title: China Reports 7.8% Growth, Close to Goal It Set for '98
1998-12-31.abstract: China says it achieved economic growth of 7.8 percent for 1998, essentially meeting 8 percent goal set by political leaders at beginning of year; many economists around world are skeptical of growth report, citing indicators such as slow growth in electricity use and declining retail prices (M)

1998-12-31.title: Just Hours Before Deadline, Mexicans Draft an Austerity Budget
1998-12-31.abstract: Mexican lawmakers reach compromise on country's most austere budget in modern times, just hours before constitutional deadline; full Congress is expected to adopt package, which includes deep spending cuts and increases in tariffs and fuel prices (M)

1998-12-31.title: World Briefing
1998-12-31.abstract: EUROPE    RUSSIA: YELTSIN ATTACKS EXTREMISM -- President Boris N. Yeltsin ordered the Government to fight political and religious extremism in the wake of several recent anti-Semitic outbursts by Russian politicians. Mr. Yeltsin told Prime Minister Yevgeny M. Primakov and other senior officials to draw up a program to oppose extremism, the President's spokesman said.   (AP)    SPAIN: CONCERN OVER BASQUES -- The conservative Government and opposition Socialist Party expressed concern that the new regional government formed by Basque nationalists could undercut the fledging peace effort in the Basque region. The crucial votes came from the leftist coalition Euskal Heritarrok, which is considered close to the Basque separatists who have observed a cease-fire since September.   (NYT)
